_comment: Canonical naming + URL config for the Trove **marketing site** (gettrove.vercel.app / trove-site-psi.vercel.app). Single source of truth — if you rename anything, edit ONLY this file, then run `bin/check-name-drift.sh` to find leftovers, and `bin/validate.sh` to confirm GitHub + Vercel + folder all agree. This config is scoped to the SITE only — the macOS app (../macos/) and the Avalonia/Windows port (../avalonia-port/) are tracked separately in their own repos. See ../macos/CLAUDE.md for the broader project handoff note.
tagline: Native macOS productivity suite — clipboard, snippets, OCR, screen capture, PDF tools, calculator, and more.
_keepReferencesIn_doc: Paths (relative to the site repo OR portfolio root) where the drift checker should IGNORE matches against previousSlugs/previousDisplayNames/previousUrls. Add entries here when adopting a new alias that legitimately mentions an old name.
notes: Folder is `site/` inside the multi-platform `trove/` monorepo, but the canonical slug for THIS deployment is `trove-site` (matches GitHub repo + Vercel project). The folder/slug mismatch is intentional — the slug is the deployment identity, the folder is the role within the monorepo.

Trove's macOS app source lives at `../macos/` with its own repo (`ArnavGoel03/trove`). Releases of the .app are tagged there; this site's DownloadButton component resolves to `https://github.com/ArnavGoel03/trove/releases/latest/download/Trove.zip` so the marketing page automatically follows whatever's newest there.
The Avalonia/Windows port stub sits at `../avalonia-port/` (formerly `Trove.App` — renamed 2026-05-31 to stop macOS Launchpad from indexing it as a fake .app).
